title: macrocarpium officinale
intro: Macrocarpium officinale, also known as the greater celandine or chelidonium, is a flowering plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The medicinal properties of Macrocarpium officinale lie in its roots, which contain alkaloids that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat various skin conditions, such as warts, eczema, and acne, as well as its potential to reduce pain and inflammation. Macrocarpium officinale can be prepared as a tincture, ointment, or salve, and it is often used in herbal medicine to treat a range of ailments.
